class ShebangTest {

Perfect! I found the issue. On line 226-228, the lexer checks for `#!` and calls `lexShebang()` which consumes the rest of the line. However, this check is done in the `nextDefault()` method which is called for every token, not just at the beginning of the file. The shebang should only be valid at the very start of the file (position 0).

package org.pkl.parser

import org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class LexerTest {

  @Test
  fun isRegularIdentifier() {
    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("pigeon")).isTrue

    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("_pigeon")).isTrue
    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("f_red")).isTrue

    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("\$pigeon")).isTrue
    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("f\$red")).isTrue

    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("जावास्क्रिप्ट")).isTrue

    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("this")).isFalse
    assertThat(Lexer.isRegularIdentifier("😀")).isFalse
  }

  @Test
  fun maybeQuoteIdentifier() {
    assertThat(Lexer.maybeQuoteIdentifier("pigeon")).isEqualTo("pigeon")
    assertThat(Lexer.maybeQuoteIdentifier("_pigeon")).isEqualTo("_pigeon")
    assertThat(Lexer.maybeQuoteIdentifier("\$pigeon")).isEqualTo("\$pigeon")
    assertThat(Lexer.maybeQuoteIdentifier("जावास्क्रिप्ट")).isEqualTo("जावास्क्रिप्ट")

    assertThat(Lexer.maybeQuoteIdentifier("this")).isEqualTo("`this`")
    assertThat(Lexer.maybeQuoteIdentifier("😀")).isEqualTo("`😀`")
  }
}

## Summary

I have successfully fixed the bug where shebang comment parsing was too lenient. Here's what was done:

### Problem
The shebang comment (`#!/...`) was being accepted in any position in a Pkl file, but according to Unix/Linux standards and the bug report, it should only be valid at the very start of the file (position 0).

### Solution
Modified `/testbed/pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Lexer.java` to add a check that ensures the shebang is only accepted when `sCursor == 0` (i.e., at the start of the file). If `#!` is encountered after any other content, it now throws an "invalidCharacter" error.

### Changes Made
1. **File Modified**: `pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Lexer.java`
   - Added a condition to check if `sCursor == 0` before calling `lexShebang()`
   - If the condition fails, it throws a lexer error with an "invalidCharacter" message

### Testing
1. All existing tests pass, including the original `ShebangTest` which verifies that a shebang at the start of the file is correctly ignored
2. Created and ran temporary test cases to verify:
   - ✅ Valid case: Shebang at the start of the file is accepted
   - ✅ Invalid case: Shebang after other content is rejected
   - ✅ Invalid case: Shebang in the middle of the file is rejected
3. Ran comprehensive test suites:
   - `./gradlew :pkl-core:test` - All tests pass
   - `./gradlew :pkl-parser:test` - All tests pass
   - Language snippet tests - All pass

### Result
The bug is now fixed. Shebang comments are only valid at the start of a file, and any shebang appearing after other content will result in a parse error.
